description: Lock longevity_escape_velocity_lev to a speculative geroscience and rejuvenation-intervention threshold, avoiding unsupported nanomedicine or organ-replacement prerequisite claims.

checks.reason: NIA aging-research portfolios support biomarkers, heterogeneity of aging, translational human research, and clinical trials as measurement and targeting context for individualized geroscience.
Geroscience and cellular-senescence reviews identify senolytics and related gerotherapeutics as direct healthspan-extension research paths, while human benefit remains unsettled.
Partial reprogramming and epigenetic rejuvenation are plausible but still safety-limited research paths toward biological rejuvenation.
Current LEV sources support geroscience, senolytics, biomarkers, and reprogramming research rather than advanced nanomedicine as a direct prerequisite.
Whole-organ replacement may be relevant to regenerative medicine but is not a source-backed direct prerequisite for the broad LEV threshold.
